entries.definition:
kh–ṭ–ʾ to miss the target, to veer away from the correct path, to err, to commit a crime, to sin; a mistake, a crime, a sin. Of this root, eight forms occur 22 times in the Qur’an: أَخْطَأ ʾakhṭaʾa twice; خَطَأ khaṭaʾ twice; خِطْء khiṭʾ once; خَاطِئُون khāṭiʾūn five times; خَاطِئَة khāṭiʾatun twice; خَطِيئَة khaṭīʾatun three times; خَطِيئَات khaṭīʾāt twice and خَطَايَا khaṭāyā five times.
أَخْطَأ ʾakhṭaʾa [v. IV, intrans.] to err, to be mistaken (33:5) وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا أَخْطَأْتُمْ بِهِ and there is no blame on you regarding the mistakes you make, only [you are blamed] for what your hearts deliberately intend.

خَطَأ khaṭaʾ [v. n./n., adverbially used] by mistake, in error (4:92) وَمَا كَانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ أَنْ يَقْتُلَ مُؤْمِنًا إِلَّا خَطَأً it is not [conceivable] for a believer to kill another believer, except by mistake.

خِطْء khiṭʾ [n./v. n. implying intensity] mistake, sin, crime, an act of disobedience (17:31) وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلَادَكُمْ خَشْيَةَ إِمْلَاقٍ نَحْنُ نَرْزُقُهُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ إِنَّ قَتْلَهُمْ كَانَ خِطْئًا كَبِيرًا do not kill your children for fear of poverty–We shall provide for them and for you–killing them is a great sin.

خَاطِئُون khāṭiʾūn [pl. of act. part. خَاطِئ khāṭiʾ; fem. خَاطِئَة khāṭiʾatun] 1 those who sin, sinners (12:97) قَالُوا يَا أَبَانَا اسْتَغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا إِنَّا كُنَّا خَاطِئِينَ they said, ‘Father, ask God to forgive us our sins–we indeed were sinners’

were indeed sinners’ 2 [of deeds] sinful, erroneous (69:9) وَجَاءَ فِرْعَوْنُ وَمَنْ قَبْلَهُ وَالْمُؤْتَفِكَاتُ بِالْخَاطِئَةِ Pharaoh, those before him, and the ruined cities–[all] committed sinful [deeds].

خَطِيئَة khaṭīʾatun [n., pl. خَطِيئَات khaṭīʾāt and خَطَايَا khaṭāyā] evildoing, sin, crime (71:25) مِمَّا خَطِيئَاتِهِمْ أُغْرِقُوا فَأُدْخِلُوا نَارًا because of their sins they were drowned and then made to enter a Fire.
